reason: Adding the nosuid mount option to /boot/efi is a standard security hardening practice that mitigates the risk of setuid program execution on the boot partition, which rarely requires SUID/SGID permissions. The remediation script includes conditional checks to prevent application in containers, OpenShift environments, or when the mount point is absent, reducing the likelihood of system breakage. This change aligns with typical STIG hardening patterns for filesystem configurations.
